name: From Vitoria to Ribera Baja — circular through the Zadorra River valley
name_original: De Vitoria a la Ribera Baja — circular por el valle del Río Zadorra
region_intro: Road cycling around Vitoria-Gasteiz is characterized by an extensive network of green spaces and generally flat topography, making it highly accessible for cyclists. The city is encircled by the Green Belt, a system of peri-urban parks offering numerous routes. Beyond the urban core, the region features flat plains and is bordered by forested mountainous ranges, providing diverse terrain for road cyclists. Best road cycling routes around Vitoria-Gasteiz The most popular road cycling route is Ullíbarri-Gamboa Reservoir - circular from Vitoria-Gasteiz, a 27.2 miles (43.8 km) trail that takes 1 hour 55 minutes to complete. This easy route offers scenic views around the reservoir. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is County of Treviño, a moderate 23.2 miles (37.3 km) path. This route showcases varied landscapes, transitioning from plains to more mountainous areas. Local road cyclists also love the Ullíbarri-Gamboa Reservoir North Loop from Vitoria-Gasteiz, a 30.6 miles (49.3 km) trail leading through the reservoir area, often completed in about 2 hours 7 minutes. Road cycling around Vitoria-Gasteiz is defined by extensive green spaces, flat plains, and surrounding forested mountainous ranges. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy loops to more challenging climbs. The routes in Vitoria-Gasteiz are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from more than 170 reviews. More than 16,000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Vitoria-Gasteiz's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Vitoria-Gasteiz?
A: There are over 190 road cycling routes around Vitoria-Gasteiz, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels. You'll find everything from easy loops to more challenging climbs through varied landscapes.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Vitoria-Gasteiz?
A: Vitoria-Gasteiz offers diverse terrain for road cyclists. The city itself and the surrounding Llanada Alavesa (Alava Plain) are generally flat, making for easy and comfortable rides. However, the wider region of Álava also features forested mountainous ranges such as the Montes de Vitoria, Sierra de Entzia, Sierra de Elgea, and Aratz, providing options for more challenging routes with elevation.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes suitable for families or beginners?
A: Yes, Vitoria-Gasteiz is very cyclist-friendly, with many routes suitable for families and beginners due to its generally flat topography. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1032811010/ullibarri-gamboa-reservoir-circular-from-vitoria-gasteiz" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ullíbarri-Gamboa Reservoir - circular from Vitoria-Gasteiz&lt;/a&gt; is an easy, scenic option, perfect for a relaxed ride. The extensive Green Belt also offers numerous well-signposted trails with minimal elevation gain.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or points of interest I can see along road cycling routes?
A: Many road cycling routes in the region pass by interesting landmarks. You could cycle past the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2989133" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of Our Lady of Estíbaliz&lt;/a&gt;, or explore sections of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1630139" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vasco-Navarro Railway Greenway&lt;/a&gt;, which features old tunnels and viaducts. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2835979" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ullíbarri-Gamboa Village and Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; area is also a popular scenic spot.

Q: Are there any routes that offer scenic views of lakes or natural wetlands?
A: Absolutely. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1032825223/ullibarri-gamboa-reservoir-north-loop-from-vitoria-gasteiz" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ullíbarri-Gamboa Reservoir North Loop from Vitoria-Gasteiz&lt;/a&gt; provides beautiful views of the reservoir. Additionally, the Salburua Park, part of the city's Green Belt, is an internationally recognized wetland system where you can cycle through picturesque landscapes home to wildlife, including red deer and migratory birds.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Vitoria-Gasteiz?
A: Vitoria-Gasteiz is generally pleasant for cycling for much of the year. Spring and autumn offer mild temperatures and beautiful scenery. Summers are typically warm and sunny, ideal for longer rides, while winters are cooler but still offer opportunities for cycling, especially on the flatter routes.

Q: How accessible are the road cycling routes from the city center, and is there good infrastructure?
A: Vitoria-Gasteiz is exceptionally well-equipped for cyclists. The city boasts over 135 km of urban cycle lanes, and the extensive Green Belt, with an additional 91 km of paths, is easily accessible from the city center. This comprehensive infrastructure ensures safe and comfortable cycling, with most of the population living very close to the cycling network.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Vitoria-Gasteiz?
A: The road cycling routes in Vitoria-Gasteiz are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 170 reviews. Cyclists often praise the extensive network of green spaces, the generally flat and accessible terrain, and the diverse landscapes that transition from urban parks to plains and mountainous areas.

Q: Are there any challenging road cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, for experienced riders seeking a challenge, routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1035891813/from-vitoria-to-ribera-baja-circular-through-the-zadorra-river-valley" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Vitoria to Ribera Baja — circular through the Zadorra River valley&lt;/a&gt; offer significant elevation gain and longer distances. The region's surrounding mountainous ranges also provide opportunities for more demanding climbs and descents.

Q: Can I find amenities like picnic areas or viewpoints along the routes?
A: Yes, many routes, especially within the Green Belt and around the Ullibarri-Gamboa Reservoir, are equipped with amenities. You'll often find picnic areas, drinking fountains, and designated viewpoints, perfect for taking a break and enjoying the scenery.

Q: Is it possible to rent bikes in Vitoria-Gasteiz?
A: Yes, bike rental services are readily available in Vitoria-Gasteiz, making it convenient for visitors to explore the city and its surrounding routes. For example, bike rental facilities are available at Garaio, near the Ullibarri-Gamboa Reservoir.

Q: What is the Green Belt of Vitoria-Gasteiz, and how does it relate to road cycling?
A: The Green Belt (Anillo Verde) is an extensive network of peri-urban parks that encircles Vitoria-Gasteiz, offering high ecological and landscape value. It provides over 30 to 91 kilometers of well-signposted trails, many of which are suitable for road cycling. It connects six major parks and offers diverse ecosystems, making it a central feature for cyclists in the region. You can learn more about it on &lt;a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Green_Belt_of_Vitoria-Gasteiz" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Wikipedia&lt;/a&gt;.
